subject: Social Science (Civics)
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze the role of the judiciary in a democratic system
Understand the structure and hierarchy of the Indian judicial system
Explain the concept of judicial independence and its importance
Evaluate how courts protect Fundamental Rights
Understand the role of Public Interest Litigation (PIL)
Develop critical thinking about justice and equality under law

lesson_plan.title: Role of the Judiciary in Democracy
topics_covered: Meaning and functions of the judiciary
Importance of justice in democratic governance
Role of courts in maintaining rule of law
student_activities: Students discuss why a country needs courts
Analyze a real-life example where courts ensured justice

lesson_plan.title: Structure of the Indian Judiciary
topics_covered: Supreme Court, High Courts and Subordinate Courts
student_activities: Create a chart showing the hierarchy of courts
Explain how cases move from lower courts to higher courts

lesson_plan.title: Independence of the Judiciary
student_activities: Debate why judges must remain independent from political influence
List factors that help maintain judicial independence

lesson_plan.title: Judicial Review and Protection of Rights
student_activities: Analyze a case where courts protected citizens' rights
Write how judicial review ensures constitutional supremacy

lesson_plan.title: Public Interest Litigation (PIL)
student_activities: Discuss situations where PIL can be filed
Write how PIL helps marginalized communities

lesson_plan.title: Application and Chapter Review
topics_covered: Role of judiciary in protecting democracy
student_activities: Write a short paragraph explaining why an independent judiciary is essential

teacher_tip: Encourage students to analyze real-life court cases to understand how the judiciary protects democracy and citizens' rights.
